mainboard.priceUsed: cut-off price = upper end of the price band (retail bids at cut-off; NII/HNI cannot use cut-off but funds are blocked at cap/upper-band price)
mainboard.boundaryRule: Categories are determined by APPLICATION VALUE = lots * lotSize * price, evaluated in whole lots. Caps are INCLUSIVE: value <= Rs 2,00,000 is Retail; Rs 2,00,000 < value <= Rs 10,00,000 is S-HNI; value > Rs 10,00,000 is B-HNI.

categories.name: Retail (RII)
categories.note: Individual/NRI applications with total bid value up to Rs 2,00,000. Min 1 lot. Bids at cut-off allowed.

categories.name: S-HNI / Small NII (sNII)
categories.reservation: 1/3 of the 15% NII quota (i.e. 5% of net offer) per SEBI ICDR (Amendment) Regulations 2022, effective for issues opening after 1 Apr 2022
categories.note: Application value more than Rs 2,00,000 and up to Rs 10,00,000. Cannot bid at cut-off.

categories.name: B-HNI / Big NII (bNII)
categories.reservation: 2/3 of the 15% NII quota (i.e. 10% of net offer)
categories.note: Application value more than Rs 10,00,000. Cannot bid at cut-off.

categories.name: QIB
categories.reservation: up to 50% of net offer (mandatory min 75% for non-profitable cos under ICDR)
categories.note: Context only; not part of the value-band lot table.

categories.name: Anchor Investor
categories.reservation: sub-set of QIB, up to 60% of QIB portion; mandatory reservation within the anchor book raised to 40% (33% mutual funds + 7% insurers/pension) effective ~30 Nov 2025
categories.note: Min application Rs 10 crore (mainboard). Context only; not part of the retail/HNI lot table.

mainboard.allotment: Draw of lots (lottery) for oversubscribed issues within Retail and each NII sub-category, allotting the minimum lot to as many unique applicants as possible.

sme.appliesTo: SME IPOs opening on/after 1 July 2025 (bidding-process changes) and DRHPs filed on/after 8 March 2025 (NII sub-categorisation). Older single-NII SME framework is OUTDATED for new issues.
sme.priceUsed: Upper band / cap price. Cut-off price bidding abolished for ALL SME categories effective 1 July 2025; every bidder places a price within the band and funds are blocked at the cap price x shares. Category determination is evaluated against cap-price value.
sme.minApplicationRule: Minimum bid = 2 LOTS with application value ABOVE Rs 2,00,000 (lot-based with a Rs 2 lakh floor, not 1 lot and not purely value-based). Source: NSE Circular NSE/IPO/68604 dated 18 June 2025. If 2 * lotValue would be <= Rs 2 lakh, lot/price is sized up so 2 lots exceed Rs 2 lakh.
sme.boundaryRule: Boundaries derived in LOTS, translated to rupees at cap price. Let L = lotShares * capPrice.

categories.name: Individual Investor (formerly RII / Retail)
categories.note: Renamed post-1-July-2025. Fixed at exactly 2 lots; no separate Rs 2 lakh ceiling. More than 2 lots moves the investor into NII. Employee category = 2 lots up to max Rs 5 lakh; Shareholder/Policyholder also min 2 lots / above Rs 2 lakh.

categories.name: Small NII / Small HNI (sNII)
categories.note: Application more than 2 lots and up to a value not exceeding Rs 10 lakh. Because SME lots are large, floor(1000000/L) can be < 3, meaning NO valid sNII bid exists and everything above Individual collapses straight into bNII.

categories.name: Big NII / Big HNI (bNII)
categories.note: Application value more than Rs 10 lakh.

categories.name: Qualified Institutional Buyer (QIB)
categories.note: Must apply for more than 2 lots. Not value-bounded for individuals.

sme.allotment: Draw of lots / lottery for the minimum bid lot first, then proportionate for any balance, within Individual and each NII sub-category (replaces old purely proportionate SME NII allotment).

formula:
perLot = lotSize * price, where price = cut-off / upper-band (cap) price.
Mainboard:
  retailMin = 1 lot
  retailMax = floor(200000 / perLot)        # largest #lots with amount <= Rs 2,00,000
  sHniMin   = retailMax + 1
  sHniMax   = floor(1000000 / perLot)        # largest #lots with amount <= Rs 10,00,000
  bHniMin   = sHniMax + 1
SME (min application = 2 lots, value > Rs 2,00,000):
  individualLots = 2
  sHniMin = 3                                # smallest integer lots > 2
  sHniMax = floor(1000000 / perLot)          # may be < 3 -> no valid sNII bid
  bHniMin = floor(1000000 / perLot) + 1
For each lot count n: shares = n * lotSize; amount = n * perLot.
roundingRule: Always ROUND DOWN (floor / integer division) to whole lots. Applications must be in whole multiples of the lot size, and crossing a cap by even a single lot pushes the bid into the next category. Caps are INCLUSIVE at the boundary: amount <= Rs 2,00,000 stays Retail; amount <= Rs 10,00,000 stays S-HNI; the next-category minimum is always the prior maximum + 1 lot.
sources: NSE Circular NSE/IPO/68604 dated 18 June 2025 (SME post-July-2025 bidding rules) - https://nsearchives.nseindia.com/content/circulars/IPO68604.pdf

SEBI (ICDR) Amendment Regulations 2022, notified 14 Jan 2022, effective for issues opening after 1 Apr 2022 (mainboard NII split into sNII 1/3 and bNII 2/3) - https://www.in.kpmg.com/taxflashnews/KPMG-Flash-News-SEBI-ICDR-Amendment-Regulations-2022.pdf

SEBI (ICDR) Amendment Regulations 2025, March 2025 (SME NII sub-categorisation aligned with mainboard) - https://www.sebi.gov.in/legal/regulations/mar-2025/securities-and-exchange-board-of-india-issue-of-capital-and-disclosure-requirements-amendment-regulations-2025_92539.html
openQuestions: For SME, the rupee boundaries depend entirely on each issue's lot size and cap price; with large SME lots, sNII can be empty (floor(1000000/perLot) < 3). The exact per-issue table must be computed at runtime from that issue's lotSize and cap price.
SME minimum-application Rs 2 lakh floor: if 2 lots <= Rs 2 lakh the regulation assumes the issuer sizes the lot/price up; behaviour for any non-conforming historical/edge issue is unspecified.
Whether a SME ground-truth worked example exists to validate the SME formula the same way AMIRCHAND validates mainboard (only the mainboard example was supplied/confirmed by the verdicts).

Anchor-book reservation changed to 40% effective ~30 Nov 2025; this affects allocation context only, not the retail/HNI lot table, but downstream consumers relying on anchor figures should note the date.
